data.content: I can see your project — the express delivery test expects `15.00m` but `CalculateShipping` returns `10.00m`. Let me start `dotnet watch test` so it re-runs on every save:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user has an MSTest project targeting .NET 8.0. They want to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload. Let me start that up for them. I can see there's a failing test — the express delivery test expects 15.00 but `CalculateShipping` returns 10.00.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so they can iterate.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-5c3d91f3368844ae87f153b4be458816 && dotnet watch test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "dotnet-watch"}

data.content: There's a missing `using System;` directive causing a build error. Let me fix that so the watch loop can run:
data.reasoningText: There's a build error - missing `using System;` for `ArgumentException`. Let me fix that first so the watch loop can run, then the tests will execute and show the actual test failure.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ **Passed:** `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5`
- ❌ **Failed:** `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expects `15.00` but gets `10.00`

The watch is active in shell `dotnet-watch`. Every time you save `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`, it will automatically rebuild and re-run the tests. To fix the failing test, update the `"express"` case from `10.00m` to `15.00m` (line 23).
